# Changelog

All notable changes to ddx-agent are recorded here.
Dates use the repo convention (`YYYY-MM-DD`); versions follow semver.

## [Unreleased]

## [v0.9.24] — 2026-04-28

Two harness-parity blockers surfaced by the first agent-vs-pi paired
beadbench run.

### Fixed

- **Pi-runner panic on `send on closed channel`** (`agent-195bb183`).
  The `mirroredEvents` goroutine in `internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go`
  was leaked: nothing closed the intermediate channel after
  `parsePiStream` returned, so an in-flight event could race
  `run()`'s deferred `close(out)` and panic. Fix: `mirroredEvents`
  now reports ownership of the mid-channel; the parser-goroutine
  closes it and waits for the mirror goroutine to drain before
  signaling done. Added `TestRunner_Execute_MirrorClosesCleanly`,
  passes 20× under `-race`.
- **Test-config isolation in `agent_test`** (`agent-27806ad5`). Tests
  calling `agent.New(ServiceOptions{})` auto-loaded the developer's
  live `~/.config/agent/config.yaml`, including provider types older
  base revisions didn't know about — making bead-verify-worktrees
  fail on `go test ./...` for reasons unrelated to the bead. New
  package-level `TestMain` (`service_testmain_test.go`) points
  `HOME` and `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` at a fresh tempdir before `m.Run()`.
  Per-test `t.Setenv("HOME", …)` calls still take precedence.

## [v0.9.23] — 2026-04-28

External-benchmark integration. First adapter to compare our harness
against the public scoreboard.

### Added

- **TerminalBench adapter** (`agent-6d6ae2f6`, PR #4). New
  `internal/benchmark/external/termbench/` package that translates
  TerminalBench task format → `agent.ServiceExecuteRequest` and
  captures `harnesses.Event` streams into ATIF v1.4 trajectory output
  the upstream verifier expects. Reads upstream `reward.txt` without
  reimplementing grading — the value of an external benchmark is
  using their rubric.
- Submodule at `scripts/benchmark/external/terminal-bench-2/` pinned
  to upstream commit `53ff2b8`. (Bead originally pointed at
  `laude-institute/terminal-bench`; that's stale per SD-008 — current
  dataset lives at terminal-bench-2.)
- Frozen 20-task subset at
  `scripts/beadbench/external/termbench-subset.json` (15 stratified
  hard/medium tasks reused from the evidence-grade comparison + 5
  easy smoke tasks).
- `cmd/bench --external=termbench` flag plus
  `cmd/bench/external_termbench.go` runner. Writes results to
  `benchmark-results/termbench/<run-id>/results.json`.
- `docs/helix/02-design/external-benchmarks.md` — pattern doc for
  adding more external benchmark adapters (SWE-bench, AgentBench,
  etc.) following the TerminalBench shape.
- `docs/research/external-benchmark-baseline-2026-04-27.md` —
  baseline matrix scaffold (3 tasks × 3 arms: ddx-agent on
  Qwen3.6-plus, codex on GPT-5.4, claude-code on Sonnet-4.6). Cells
  marked `_pending_` until the verifier-side run lands.

### Honestly-scoped v1 gaps

- The adapter does not invoke Harbor / pytest / Docker. It writes
  the artifacts the verifier expects; actual scoring remains
  `scripts/benchmark/run_benchmark.sh`'s job.
- Baseline doc cells are placeholders pending verifier-side run.

## [v0.9.22] — 2026-04-28

Refactor + reliability release. Closes the architectural smell that
caused v0.9.18-.20's lucebox/vllm shipping bug, lays down the
internal-benchmark-corpus foundation, and unblocks lucebox conformance
on thinking-mode locals.

### Refactor

- **Provider registry** (`agent-8e4eb44c`). Single source of truth for
  provider-type → factory mappings in `internal/provider/registry/`.
  Each provider package registers a Descriptor in `init()`. The two
  parallel factories (`internal/config/config.go:buildProviderFromConfig`
  and `service_native_provider.go:buildNativeProvider`) collapse to
  a `registry.Lookup(...)` + `Factory(Inputs{...})`. 11 types
  registered (anthropic, lmstudio, lucebox, minimax, ollama, omlx,
  openai, openrouter, qwen, vllm, zai). Round-trip test in
  `registry_test.go` enumerates expected types and asserts every one
  resolves and produces a non-nil provider — catches drift on import.
  Independent fresh-eyes review verified no information loss.

### Added

- **Internal benchmark corpus + curation flow** (bead `agent-4f03f33d`).
  New `internal/corpus` package + `ddx-agent corpus
  promote|validate|list` subcommand stand up a capability-tagged
  corpus of closed beads worth regression-tracking. Files live under
  `scripts/beadbench/corpus.yaml`, `scripts/beadbench/corpus/<bead-id>.yaml`,

commit 7d50a792ee5bc77d27edbf203fc463ad71ab3c6d
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 28 20:29:49 2026 -0400

    docs: document ddx-agent v0.9.23 routing point release [ddx-967e9802]
    
    Add CHANGELOG.md with a routing point release entry citing each consumed
    upstream commit and bead. Satisfies AC #1 of ddx-fdd3ea36 alongside the
    existing cli/go.mod pin at github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23.

 CHANGELOG.md | 34 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 34 insertions(+)

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.
4	
5	## [Unreleased]
6	
7	### Routing point release — ddx-agent v0.9.23
8	
9	DDx now consumes the cumulative routing contract delivered upstream across
10	`github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` v0.9.10..v0.9.24 (pinned at v0.9.23 in
11	`cli/go.mod`). Upstream shipped these capabilities incrementally across
12	multiple tags rather than as a single release, so this entry bundles the
13	combined contract DDx now relies on.
14	
15	Consumed upstream capabilities (commit hash · upstream bead):
16	
17	- **Cost-aware routing tiebreak** — `b85e90e` · [agent-0dafc7f0](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl) (regression coverage tightened in `9c80eb1`). Adds cost as a routing dimension so equally-capable candidates resolve to the cheaper provider.
18	- **First-class routing profiles** — `b645280` · [agent-191a74f9](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Ships the `default`, `local`, and `standard` profiles with zero-interaction defaults.
19	- **Routing reference docs** — `faafff2`, `db26108` · [agent-1f46cf22](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Publishes the routing reference and clarifies the best-provider cost baseline.
20	- **Public route decision trace** — `0bc2bf4` · [agent-53f38d95](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Exposes the candidate decision trace so callers can inspect why a route was chosen.
21	- **Supported-models allow-list** — `fd71e00` · [agent-bab52778](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Gates exact pins by the harness allow-list so unsupported models fail fast.
22	- **Typed explicit-pin errors** — `5992c7b` · [agent-dfabb10b](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Returns typed errors for explicit-pin routing failures instead of opaque strings.
23	
24	The combined contract DDx consumes:
25	
26	- **Zero-interaction defaults** via the `default` profile.
27	- **Profiles** (`default`, `local`, `standard`) selectable without bespoke config.
28	- **Override semantics** with explicit pins gated by the supported-models allow-list and surfaced through typed errors.
29	- **Public decision trace** for inspecting candidate selection.
30	- **Cost dimension** as a tiebreak in routing decisions.
31	
32	This entry, together with the `cli/go.mod` pin at v0.9.23, satisfies AC #1 of
33	parent bead `ddx-fdd3ea36` — there is no single upstream release tag that
34	bundles the routing point release, so traceability lives here.
35

1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by
7	// RunWithConfigViaService for `ddx agent run`.
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11		"fmt"
12	
13		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15	)
16	
17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can pin one knob for
34	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		if svc == nil {
40			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
41			if err != nil {
42				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
43			}
44			svc = built
45		}
46		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
47	}
48	
49	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
50	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
51	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
52	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
53	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
54		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
55		if harness == "" {
56			harness = rcfg.Harness()
57		}
58		model := runtime.ModelOverride
59		if model == "" {
60			model = rcfg.Model()
61		}
62		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
63		if permissions == "" {
64			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
65		}
66		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
67		if sessionLogDir == "" {
68			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
69		}
70	
71		var opts RunArgs
72		opts.Context = ctx
73		opts.Harness = harness
74		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
75		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
76		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
77		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
78		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
79		opts.Model = model
80		opts.Provider = rcfg.Provider()
81		opts.ModelRef = rcfg.ModelRef()
82		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
83		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
84		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
85		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
86		opts.Permissions = permissions
87		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
88		return opts
89	}

input.content:
Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer)
Attempt: 20260429T003007-e136e75e (no-op)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
================
- AC #1 is satisfied by prior work on this branch:
  * cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at v0.9.23 (verified).
  * CHANGELOG.md (commit 7d50a792) documents the consumed upstream
    capabilities and cites each upstream commit + bead. The changelog
    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally
    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.
  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in
    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:
      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)
      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)
      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)
      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)
      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)
  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at
    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

NO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.

Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260429T003007-e136e75e/no_changes_rationale.txt`. No commit.

Summary:
- **AC #1 already done** on this branch: `cli/go.mod` pins `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23` and prior commit `7d50a792` documents the consumed upstream capabilities in `CHANGELOG.md`. All six upstream `routing-plan` beads are closed; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` and the public decision-trace surface are present in the pinned module.
- **AC #2..#10 not attempted**: the remaining DDx-side work (D1–D5 + four new test suites + Playwright golden + breaking config migration + dead-code audit) is an epic-sized, multi-subsystem refactor that the bead's own framing calls a "DDx-side tracker." Landing it as one commit risks a half-rewired dispatch path, a schema-break without the migration warnings firing, or a Playwright fixture that silently passes.
- The rationale recommends decomposing `ddx-fdd3ea36` into ~9 sub-beads (D1, D2a, D3, D4, D5, Audit, Regression, Playwright, Docs) and keeping this bead open as the umbrella tracker.
